## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BP

3	## Shape
4	
5	Grab-bag cleanup heat. Theme: consolidate to the single rightful owner — remove capability and constants that are redundantly held in more than one place. Collects post-cutover RBK cleanups of that nature.
6	
7	The constant-projection thread is the bulk of the heat. A survey found the co-maintained set is broader than colophons alone — colophons, credential roles, fixture names, .env filenames, operation verbs, and container roles all live in bash and are hand-copied into the rbtd Rust manifest or lib.rs, kept in sync only by a runtime drift check. The work points every co-maintained group through bash→Rust generation, retiring the runtime drift check in favor of a build-time diff gate.
8	
9	## Locked decisions
10	
11	- **Work that reads RBCC or the moorings layout is gated on ₣BK (moorings cutover) closing** — stable RBCC, settled layout, RBBC aliases retired. Cleanups touching neither are free to run.
12	- **Constant-projection scope: single canonical bash author, or out.** A constant is projectable to another language only if exactly one bash module canonically owns it. Constants whose identity is distributed across bash, disk, and Rust are excluded unless a separate canonical-author decision is taken first.
13	- **One generated landing file, one minted prefix `RBTDGC_`, fed per-pair.** All five co-maintained groups (colophons, RBCC set, fixtures, operation verbs, container roles) project into a single generated Rust file in the rbtd crate — new file, not new crate — under the `RBTDGC_` prefix. The consts never carry a bash module prefix (`RBZ_`, `RBCC_`, … are bash-only namespaces; stamping them on a Rust const is a false ownership claim). The cross-language correspondence is mechanical and fully generated: Rust const = `RBTDGC_` + the bash const's stem (its bash-module prefix stripped, stem carried verbatim) — one uniform rule, no per-entry mapping, hence no drift. A shared RBK-ignorant BUK primitive formats one `pub const` line; each group's canonical bash home loops its own data through it (bash 3.2 has no clean way to pass a pair-list to a function, but per-pair scalar calls are trivial). The legacy curated names (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*`, the lib.rs path macros, etc.) carry no preservation obligation — each is retired as its group's generated consts land.
14	- **Full co-maintained set is in scope** — not just colophons. Groups with a single bash home are projected directly: colophons (rbz), the RBCC tinder set incl. credential roles and .env filenames (rbcc), fixture names (rbte). Groups scattered across bash today — operation verbs and container roles — earned a canonical-author tidy first (one bash home in rbcc), then project. This honors the single-author rule rather than weakening it.
15	
16	## What done looks like
17	
18	The redundancies in scope are gone: no capability that has a better-owned home elsewhere survives in two places, and no co-maintained constant group is hand-maintained on both sides of the bash↔Rust boundary. # jjezs_paddock ₣BP

## Shape

Grab-bag cleanup heat. Theme: consolidate to the single rightful owner — remove capability and constants that are redundantly held in more than one place. Collects post-cutover RBK cleanups of that nature.

The constant-projection thread is the bulk of the heat. A survey found the co-maintained set is broader than colophons alone — colophons, credential roles, fixture names, .env filenames, operation verbs, and container roles all live in bash and are hand-copied into the rbtd Rust manifest or lib.rs, kept in sync only by a runtime drift check. The work points every co-maintained group through bash→Rust generation, retiring the runtime drift check in favor of a build-time diff gate.

## Locked decisions

- **Work that reads RBCC or the moorings layout is gated on ₣BK (moorings cutover) closing** — stable RBCC, settled layout, RBBC aliases retired. Cleanups touching neither are free to run.
- **Constant-projection scope: single canonical bash author, or out.** A constant is projectable to another language only if exactly one bash module canonically owns it. Constants whose identity is distributed across bash, disk, and Rust are excluded unless a separate canonical-author decision is taken first.
- **One generated landing file, one minted prefix `RBTDGC_`, fed per-pair.** All five co-maintained groups (colophons, RBCC set, fixtures, operation verbs, container roles) project into a single generated Rust file in the rbtd crate — new file, not new crate — under the `RBTDGC_` prefix. The consts never carry a bash module prefix (`RBZ_`, `RBCC_`, … are bash-only namespaces; stamping them on a Rust const is a false ownership claim). The cross-language correspondence is mechanical and fully generated: Rust const = `RBTDGC_` + the bash const's stem (its bash-module prefix stripped, stem carried verbatim) — one uniform rule, no per-entry mapping, hence no drift. A shared RBK-ignorant BUK primitive formats one `pub const` line; each group's canonical bash home loops its own data through it (bash 3.2 has no clean way to pass a pair-list to a function, but per-pair scalar calls are trivial). The legacy curated names (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*`, the lib.rs path macros, etc.) carry no preservation obligation — each is retired as its group's generated consts land.
- **Full co-maintained set is in scope** — not just colophons. Groups with a single bash home are projected directly: colophons (rbz), the RBCC tinder set incl. credential roles and .env filenames (rbcc), fixture names (rbte). Groups scattered across bash today — operation verbs and container roles — earned a canonical-author tidy first (one bash home in rbcc), then project. This honors the single-author rule rather than weakening it.

## What done looks like

The redundancies in scope are gone: no capability that has a better-owned home elsewhere survives in two places, and no co-maintained constant group is hand-maintained on both sides of the bash↔Rust boundary. The `RBTDGC_` generated file feeds the rbtd manifest's former hand-mirror groups and the lib.rs path macros; the runtime drift check is retired in favor of a build-time diff gate (the same fresh-generate-and-diff staleness check the qualifier already applies to the generated markdown context).

# jjezs_pace ₢BPAAD

## Character

Mechanical but broad — a consumer rename across the rbtd Rust sources.

## Goal

Migrate every colophon consumer off the hand-mirror `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*` and inline colophon literals onto the generated `RBTDGC_*` names, retiring the hand-written colophon consts. The runtime drift check stays alive here.

## Shape

- The generated colophon consts already exist (emitter pace). This pace retires the curated `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*` names and points consumers at `RBTDGC_*`.
- Discover sites with `grep -rln "RBTDRM_COLOPHON" Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/` plus the inline colophon literals (e.g. the fast suite).
- Repoint `rbtdrm_required_colophons` to the generated names but keep `rbtdrm_verify` running — do NOT retire verify here; that belongs with the diff-gate landing so drift protection is continuous.

I'm oriented. Here's the mount.

## ₢BPAAD — colophon-codegen-projection

**Goal in one sentence:** Retire the hand-written `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*` mirror and repoint every theurge consumer (and the inline colophon literals in the fast/pristine suites) onto the build-generated `RBTDGC_*` consts — while keeping `rbtdrm_verify` running against the generated names so drift protection stays continuous.

**Heat context:** `rbk-13-mvp-redundancy-cleanup` (₣BP, 3/8) — consolidate co-maintained things to a single rightful owner. The emitter pace (₢BPAAC) already landed `rbtdgc_consts.rs` (107 generated consts, wired as `pub mod rbtdgc_consts` in lib.rs). This pace is the consumer side of that projection: colophons only — fixtures, operation verbs, container roles project in later paces.

**Grounded survey:**
- **60 `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*` defs** in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs` (lines 23–126), all matched by value to an existing `RBTDGC_*` const. The rename is *not* a prefix swap — stems differ (`RBTDRM_COLOPHON_CHARGE`→`RBTDGC_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_GOV_MANTLE`→`RBTDGC_MANTLE_GOVERNOR`, `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_DEPOT_LEVY`→`RBTDGC_LEVY_DEPOT`). I'll build the authoritative map by joining the two files on the shared `"rbw-XX"` value.
- **164 consumer occurrences** across 11 files, each importing specific names from `rbtdrm_manifest`. Imports need surgery: colophon names move to a `rbtdgc_consts` use-line; the co-located `RBTDRM_ROLE_*`/`RBTDRM_FIXTURE_*`/`rbtdrm_credential_check_colophon` imports stay (out of scope).
- **Inline invocation literals** in `rbtdrf_fast.rs` (`rbw-rrv`, `rbw-rvv`, `rbw-rnv`, `rbw-rnr`, `rbw-rrr`, `rbw-rvr`, `rbw-rpr`, `rbw-rpv`, `rbw-dU`, `rbw-dl`) and `rbtdrp_pristine.rs` (`rbw-dU`) at `rbtdrf_run_tt`/`find_tabtarget_global` call sites — all have generated consts.

**Approach:**
1. Derive the by-value rename map; bulk-apply the name substitution across the 11 consumer files (scripted, since it's a strict 1:1 table).
2. Fix imports: pull colophon names out of each `rbtdrm_manifest` use-list into a `rbtdgc_consts` use-line, leaving role/fixture/verify imports intact.
3. In `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`: delete the 60 colophon consts; repoint `rbtdrm_required_colophons` and `rbtdrm_credential_check_colophon` bodies to `RBTDGC_*`; **keep `rbtdrm_verify`**.
4. Convert the fast/pristine invocation literals to `RBTDGC_*`.
5. `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` then `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh` — green is the gate.

**One decision for you** — two classes of literal I'd *leave* as strings, not convert:
- **Diagnostic prose** like `"rbw-dU exited 0 with no argument…"` and the `combined.contains("rbw-dl")` output assertion — human-facing message text / output matching, not invocation targets.
- **Test fixtures** in `rbtdti_invocation.rs` (`"rbw-cb.Bark.tadmor.sh"`) — synthetic tabtarget filenames exercising the name-matching finder, not the bark operation; converting them would misrepresent the test.

**Name assessment:** the silks `colophon-codegen-projection` reads like the emitter pace; this is the *consumer migration*. Optional `jjx_relabel` to e.g. `colophon-consumer-migration` or `retire-colophon-mirror`.

**Strategy:** Sequential, single session here — the cross-file consistency + import surgery + build-loop verification want one coherent pass, not parallel agents. Mechanical but the mapping is sharp-edged, so I'll verify with the build rather than trust the substitution.

Want me to proceed? And do you agree with leaving the prose/test-fixture literals as-is, and with the rename?

input.command:
cd /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster
python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import re, glob, os
# Build map by value-join
gc = {}
for line in open("Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdgc_consts.rs"):
    m = re.match(r'pub const (RBTDGC_\w+): &str = "([^"]+)";', line)
    if m: gc[m.group(2)] = m.group(1)
namemap = {}   # RBTDRM_COLOPHON_X -> RBTDGC_Y
valmap  = {}   # "rbw-XX" -> RBTDGC_Y  (for literal interpolation later)
for line in open("Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs"):
    m = re.match(r'pub const (RBTDRM_COLOPHON_\w+): &str = "([^"]+)";', line)
    if m:
        namemap[m.group(1)] = gc[m.group(2)]
        valmap[m.group(2)] = gc[m.group(2)]
# Persist valmap for the literal-interpolation step
import json
json.dump(valmap, open("/tmp/colophon_valmap.json","w"), indent=0)

# Token-substitute RBTDRM_COLOPHON_X -> RBTDGC_Y across consumers (whole-word)
skip = {"rbtdrm_manifest.rs", "rbtdgc_consts.rs"}
# longest-first to avoid prefix shadowing
keys = sorted(namemap, key=len, reverse=True)
pat = re.compile(r'\b(' + '|'.join(keys) + r')\b')
changed = []
for path in glob.glob("Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/*.rs"):
    if os.path.basename(path) in skip: continue
    src = open(path).read()
    new = pat.sub(lambda m: namemap[m.group(1)], src)
    if new != src:
        open(path,"w").write(new)
        changed.append((os.path.basename(path), src.count("RBTDRM_COLOPHON_")))
for n,c in sorted(changed): print(f"{n}: {c} colophon refs renamed")
print(f"valmap entries: {len(valmap)}")
PYEOF

17	// RBTDRI — tabtarget invocation layer for theurge
18	//
19	// Theurge invokes bottle operations exclusively through tabtargets, never
20	// reimplementing bash command logic. This module provides:
21	//
22	//   1. Tabtarget discovery — imprint-scoped, global, or nameplate-scoped
23	//   2. Tabtarget execution with BURV isolation — per-invocation output/temp dirs
24	//   3. Ifrit verdict parsing — extract verdict from ifrit stdout + exit code

37	/// BUK dispatch output subdirectory — tabtargets write facts to BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR/current.
38	/// Matches BURD_OUTPUT_DIR = "${BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR}/current" from bud_dispatch.sh.
39	pub const RBTDRI_BURV_OUTPUT_SUBDIR: &str = "current";

41	/// Env var name read by `buc_require` (buc_command.sh:335) to bypass interactive

45	/// Value paired with `RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY` to skip the confirmation prompt.

48	/// BUK dispatch env var carrying the temp root for theurge — anchors BURV
49	/// per-invoke temp dirs under temp-buk/ rather than /tmp/. Required: theurge
50	/// fails at startup if unset. Set by bud_dispatch.sh on every tabtarget call.
51	pub const RBTDRI_BURD_TEMP_DIR_KEY: &str = "BURD_TEMP_DIR";
52	
53	/// BURV invoke-directory name from a zero-based invoke count.

56	/// this rather than hand-expanding the literal.

82	/// canonical-*, etc.) carry their fixture name in this slot too, but no
83	/// nameplate-shaped consumer reads them.
84	pub struct rbtdri_Context {

98	    ) -> Self {

102	            burv_temp_root: burv_temp_root.to_path_buf(),
103	            burv_output_root: burv_output_root.to_path_buf(),

114	    }
115	}

117	// ── Tabtarget discovery ──────────────────────────────────────
118	
119	/// Find the tabtarget script for a colophon + imprint (nameplate or role).
120	///
121	/// Scans tt/ for files matching `{colophon}.*.{imprint}.sh`.
122	/// Returns error if zero or multiple matches — exactly one must exist.
123	pub fn rbtdri_find_tabtarget(
124	    project_root: &Path,
125	    colophon: &str,
126	    imprint: &str,
127	) -> Result<PathBuf, String> {
128	    let tt_dir = project_root.join("tt");
129	    let prefix = format!("{}.", colophon);
130	    let suffix = format!(".{}.sh", imprint);
131	
132	    let entries = std::fs::read_dir(&tt_dir)
133	        .map_err(|e| format!("rbtdri: cannot read tt/ directory: {}", e))?;
134	
135	    let matches: Vec<PathBuf> = entries
136	        .filter_map(|entry| entry.ok())
137	        .map(|entry| entry.path())
138	        .filter(|path| {
139	            if let Some(name) = path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {
140	                name.starts_with(&prefix) && name.ends_with(&suffix)
141	            } else {
142	                false
143	            }
144	        })
145	        .collect();
146	
147	    match matches.len() {
148	        0 => Err(format!(
149	            "rbtdri: no tabtarget for colophon '{}' imprint '{}'",
150	            colophon, imprint
151	        )),
152	        1 => Ok(matches.into_iter().next().unwrap()),
153	        n => Err(format!(
154	            "rbtdri: {} tabtargets match colophon '{}' imprint '{}' — expected exactly one",
155	            n, colophon, imprint
156	        )),
157	    }
158	}
159	
160	/// Find a global tabtarget (no imprint suffix).
161	///
162	/// Scans tt/ for files matching `{colophon}.{frontispiece}.sh` (exactly two dots).
163	/// Rejects files with imprint suffixes (three+ dots).
164	pub fn rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global(
165	    project_root: &Path,
166	    colophon: &str,
167	) -> Result<PathBuf, String> {
168	    let tt_dir = project_root.join("tt");
169	    let prefix = format!("{}.", colophon);
170	
171	    let entries = std::fs::read_dir(&tt_dir)
172	        .map_err(|e| format!("rbtdri: cannot read tt/ directory: {}", e))?;
173	
174	    let matches: Vec<PathBuf> = entries
175	        .filter_map(|entry| entry.ok())
176	        .map(|entry| entry.path())
177	        .filter(|path| {
178	            if let Some(name) = path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {
179	                if name.starts_with(&prefix) && name.ends_with(".sh") {
180	                    // Global: no imprint — exactly one part between colophon and .sh
181	                    let middle = &name[prefix.len()..name.len() - 3]; // strip ".sh"
182	                    !middle.contains('.')
183	                } else {
184	                    false
185	                }
186	            } else {
187	                false
188	            }
189	        })
190	        .collect();
191	
192	    match matches.len() {
193	        0 => Err(format!(
194	            "rbtdri: no global tabtarget for colophon '{}'",
195	            colophon
196	        )),
197	        1 => Ok(matches.into_iter().next().unwrap()),
198	        n => Err(format!(
199	            "rbtdri: {} global tabtargets match colophon '{}' — expected exactly one",
200	            n, colophon
201	        )),
202	    }
203	}
